cases.title: Antioch Session v. Eastern Carolina Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Antioch Session complained that Eastern Carolina Presbytery erred in licensing Arthur Peterson given his divorce/remarriage history and his views on creation and a local flood. The SJC did not sustain the complaint on Issue 1 (licensure approval, 13-4), finding ECP acted with constitutional thoroughness and that precedent permitted a divorced man to hold office under certain conditions, but did sustain Issue 2 (3-0 panel; 17-0 SJC), finding ECP negligent in allowing Peterson to serve as stated supply for over six months before licensing him.
